rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.title: Lion Optimizer
results.note: Lion uses sign-based momentum (same-direction sign accumulation), not an opposite-sign counterweight accumulator; no bistable lever-balance update; no shaduf/counterweight vocabulary; uniform-magnitude sign updates differ functionally from passive anti-gradient damping with separate opposite-direction reserve.

results.title: Lion: Evolved Sign Momentum
results.note: Sign of weighted-average momentum plus current gradient; same-direction sign EMA, not a counterweight that grows specifically on opposite-sign gradients. No bistable/lever interpretation. Functionally distinct update rule.

results.title: Lion Secretly Solves Constrained Optimization (Lyapunov 2023/10)
results.note: Lyapunov/constrained-optimization view of Lion; no opposite-sign counterweight accumulator, no bistable rest-position dynamics, no passive anti-oscillation reserve. Different mathematical framing.

results.title: Symbolic Discovery of Optimization Algorithms (Lion paper)
results.note: Symbolic-search-discovered sign-momentum + gradient combination. No explicit opposite-sign reserve term, no lever-balance interpretation, no bistability between rest states. Mechanism differs.

results.title: Complete Guide to Neural Network Optimizers (2026/01)
results.note: General survey mentioning momentum cancellation damps oscillation via positive/negative gradient averaging. Closer functionally because oscillation damping via opposite signs is the same goal, but standard momentum's cancellation is implicit in a single EMA — not a separate opposite-sign counterweight accumulator gated on sign(g_t) != sign(c_{t-1}) with bistable lever dynamics. Below 0.7 threshold.

results.title: Awesome Second-Order Optimization
results.note: Curated list of second-order/Muon methods; not about sign-aware opposite-direction accumulation or counterweight dynamics. Distant.

results.title: Refined Lion Optimizer for Deep Learning (Sci Reports 2025)
results.note: Refines Lion sign-momentum for stability; still same-direction sign EMA, no separate opposite-sign counterweight reserve, no bistable lever-balance update rule. Different mechanism.

results.title: Momentum (Cornell Wiki)
results.note: Describes momentum damping oscillation through cancellation of opposing gradients in a single EMA. Functionally adjacent — anti-oscillation via opposite-sign interaction — but classical momentum accumulates same-direction history and damps oscillation implicitly; it does not maintain a separate opposite-sign counterweight c_t gated on sign disagreement, nor a bistable lever-balance update u_t = lr*(g_t - gamma*c_t*sign(g_{t-1})). Stays below 0.7.
